title: Busan Boat Rental: What You Need to Know in 2026
html:
Exploring Busan by boat is one of the best ways to experience South Korea's second city. Whether you depart from Haeundae Beach or Suyeong Bay, expect to pay from 200 to 2,500 EUR for a half-day rental, depending on the vessel. BoatHire24 connects you with local owners for a smooth booking experience, so you can compare options for a yacht charter Busan easily.

Where to set off in Busan?

The best departure points are Haeundae Beach, Gwangalli Marina, Suyeong Bay, Dadaepo Beach, and the harbour at Busan Yacht Center. Each offers easy access to the open sea and sheltered anchorages. Haeundae is the most popular, with several marinas and water taxi services. Gwangalli Marina provides a quiet base close to Gwangan Bridge. Suyeong Bay, home to the Busan Sailing Federation, is ideal for keelboats and catamarans. Dadaepo, at the western edge, gives quick access to the Nakdong River estuary. You can yacht charter Busan for your chosen dates.

Which boat to rent?

Boat choice depends on your group size, budget, and what you want to do on the water. Here are the main options available in Busan.

  1. Small motorboat (e.g. Axopar 28), perfect for a couple or four friends exploring the coastline, with a top speed of 35 knots. From 300 EUR per half-day.
  2. Family cruiser (e.g. Fairline 45), comfortable for a family of six to eight, with a cabin and sundeck. From 800 EUR per half-day. For full details see the Fairline listing on BoatHire24.
  3. Catamaran (e.g. Lagoon 42), stable and spacious, ideal for a group of 8, 10, with a shallow draft for coastal cruising. From 1,200 EUR per half-day.
  4. Luxury motor yacht (e.g. Azimut 55), ultimate comfort for up to 12 guests, with a flybridge and crew. From 2,500 EUR per half-day.
  5. Sailing yachts (e.g. Sunseeker 50), for those who prefer wind power, typically sleeps 6, 8 and includes a skipper. From 900 EUR per half-day.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Busan?

Prices vary widely, but you can expect to pay between 200 and 2,500 EUR for a half-day rental, depending on boat type and season. The table below gives a quick breakdown. One option worth a look is the Fairline listing.

  • Shared tour (2 hours, up to 12 guests): from 50 EUR per person.
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours, up to 6 guests): 250, 500 EUR.
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hours, up to 12 guests): 1,500, 2,000 EUR.
  • Crewed luxury yacht half-day (4 hours, up to 12 guests): 1,800, 2,500 EUR.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Busan?

Most boats require a Korean Powerboat Licence or an International Certificate of Competence (ICC). However, many charters provide a skipper, so you can simply relax and enjoy the trip. Always confirm with the operator, the team at Gwangalli Marina can advise.

Yes, a licence is required for bareboat charters, but you can always hire a skipper if you don't have one. International visitors with an ICC are generally accepted. The high season runs from July to August, when the monsoon wind (jangma) brings occasional squalls, so checking the weather forecast is essential.

At a glance

  • Price band: 200, 2,500 EUR (half-day)
  • Peak months: July, August
  • Typical trip length: 2, 8 hours
  • Maximum guests: 12
  • Currency: EUR
  • Boat types: motor yachts, catamarans, sailing yachts

Questions about boat rental in Busan

Can I rent a boat for a day trip to Oryukdo Island?

Yes, many charters offer trips to Oryukdo, which is about 5 nautical miles from Haeundae, taking roughly 30 minutes each way.

Are there any options for fishing from a rental boat?

Yes, sport fishing is popular in the Korea Strait, and many motorboats come equipped with rod holders and fish finders.

What should I bring for a day on the water in Busan?

Bring sunscreen, a light jacket, snacks and drinks, most boats supply safety gear, life jackets, and a full tank of fuel.

Can I celebrate a birthday or party on a rented boat?

Yes, many charter operators, especially those based at Suyeong Bay, welcome small celebrations for up to 12 guests, often with catering options.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Busan or list your own. If you own a boat in Busan, visit boathire24.com to list your boat and start earning income in 2026.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Busan and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
